comments: A croissant should be nice and crispy, cut it open as soon as it comes out of the oven, and spread the new AH Terra Pistachio paste in it, optionally with or without butter and/or press it in a waffle iron!
A nice warm freshly baked croissant with jam & cheese. Jam for the sweetness & the cheese for the savouryness

My ultimate croissant topping? A layer of lemon cream, fresh strawberries cut into heart shapes on top, and a cloud of lavender honey as a finishing touch. This is how it smells and tastes like spring on a French terrace. So delicious that you would even want to keep the crumbs!
a delicious croissant with feta and rocket

A home-baked croissant is the tastiest. Before you roll up the croissant completely, it is delicious to put the ah paashagel on it. Then the chocolate sprinkles can melt nicely in the oven, and you still have the special easter bunnies (from the ah paashagel) on your croissant to make it festive.
The tastiest topping for a croissant for me is with a piece of ham and cheese with a layer of butter...uhhhhh yummy
A croissant is more than just a snack – you can really do anything with it!
Nothing beats a delicious croissant with sausage! I enjoy it every time
I prefer to eat a croissant with pandan cream, easy to prepare and also tasty
Preferably topped with ham and cheese and then briefly in the oven or under the sandwich maker. Very tasty.
This weekend with Easter made delicious croissants with pastry cream, fresh strawberries and fresh whipped cream, finished with powdered sugar! It was a great success!
Yesterday with the Easter brunch in a nice brasserie we got among other things Oeufs Bénédicte on a croissant with Jambon and Hollandaise sauce. That was so tasty, nice to try it yourself.....

Crossiant should always be warmed up, about 10 seconds in the microwave is enough (assuming it is on 800 w), then cut from the middle -> with pistachio cream + fresh thin pieces of strawberry is how you turn a normal bun into something five star dessert :D

I prefer to top my croissant with light products. For example, the hero strawberry jam zero (from the ah ofc). This way I save calories but I can still enjoy my delicious croissant. Besides working at the ah, I am often & enjoy sports. And what is nicer than being able to flop down on the XXL beanbag after sports? With my XXL nutrition shake! Greetingsss
I top my croissant with a layer of marshmallow fluff first and a second layer of Nutella. Sweet and savory is always the perfect combination

I top my croissant with a layer of spicy hummus first, a few slices of chicken breast second layer, avocado smash third layer and some salt and pepper and finally the 4th layer: cottage cheese and some salt and pepper again. Enjoy!
I prefer to top a nice, greasy croissant with a nice, greasy profit share from Albert Heijn. ;)
My favorite croissant combination is with prosciutto, goat cheese, pesto, honey and pine nuts. Nice and fat just like this mega croissant. That is the best from Albert Heijn.
I've fortunately seen it pass by more often so I'm not the only one, but I prefer to eat it straight from the oven without anything on it, then it's the tastiest
My perfect sweet croissant? Filled with raspberry compote, a swirl of pistachio cream and topped with white chocolate flakes and powdered sugar. Like tasting a summery French pastry in one bite!”
Banana, Nutella and walnuts or pecans
Salmon with cream cheese, then I'm like a celebrity. Gives a bit of that luxury vibe... Which I am of course ;)

My sister would be super happy with this super sweet croissant for her student house. Our specialty: croissant with prosciutto, parmesan flakes, fig jam and pine nuts. The parmesan melts slightly, the prosciutto gives a salty bite and the fig jam provides a sweet-savory balance.

This weekend I proposed a fun idea for Easter breakfast to my family! I came up with the idea to top the warm croissants my mother made for us with raspberries and blackberries! I got the idea because hamsters eat that too, and I like to stock up at Albert Heijn. How convenient.

A delicious butter croissant topped with salmon and cream cheese is a delicious combination for me, at lunchtime you make me very happy with this! Then I can go for it again in the afternoon, that we have the bread department in perfect condition again. This big cuddly croissant gets a nice place with us, the kids think it's great anyway!
I keep it nice and simple: warm croissant, sardines in olive oil and a few drops of lemon juice. That's all you need for a next level breakfast. A real "You have to try it to understand it" snack
I like to eat my croissant with avocado, truffle mayonnaise, smoked chicken and romaine lettuce.
My favorite is a fresh croissant with ham, cheese and pineapple, preferably grilled.

for breakfast I go for a warm petit beurre croissant with cottage cheese, strawberries and melted salted caramel chocolate. if I also crave a croissant for lunch, I go for a cretzel with smoked salmon, arugula, cream cheese, sliced cucumbers and soy or teriyaki sauce on top. both extremely tasty but only when shared with others and served on a croissant shaped cutting board.
I like to make my own croissants and I always put pieces of banana with chocolate spread in them! Really recommended!
I usually eat it with peanut butter and chocolate sprinkles. Really recommended for anyone without money.
I usually add chicken fillet and cheese (whatever we have in the fridge but preferably extra mature) with chicken spices. Then I put it on the sandwich maker
A lukewarm croissant with cheese, chicken fillet, egg and salt and pepper of course

I really like it when I have a warm croissant just out of the oven with a little cheese and chicken so that the cheese melts, I wait a while before eating it so that the cheese has melted properly, and to let all the stress flow away from me, it has to be warm.
Jam and Nutella
I like to put a little Nutella + strawberry + crystal sugar on the frozen croissini's mini croissants.

I like to spread peanut butter on my croissant, then put slices of banana on top and top it off with cinnamon. Then dip it all in the quark. This gives you a nice (and I find tasty) combination of healthy fats, proteins, fibers, vitamins and a sweet taste.

The tastiest topping on a croissant, in my opinion, is goat cheese with lavender honey and fresh blueberries. The creamy, slightly salty taste of the goat cheese combines perfectly with the floral sweetness of the honey. The blueberries provide a fresh counterpart and make the whole surprising and balanced. This combination gives the croissant a luxurious and special twist, reminiscent of the flavors of the South of France.
'Dubai chocolate spread', because it's such a trend.
Pistachio cream because then it looks like I'm really following the trends
salami is so tasty on it
I always dip my croissant in pumpkin soup. A strange combination perhaps, but definitely worth a try
I don't put anything on my croissant because I want him to know that I love him just the way he is!
I top my croissant with stress, deadlines and a dash of self-pity. Tastes like studying during exam week.
Croissant with rose petal jam, white chocolate and almond shavings or croissant with brie/camembert cheese, grapes, honey and walnuts <3
The croissant with vanilla ice cream, crispy bacon and a generous drizzle of maple syrup!
I like to eat my croissant with filet americano.
I prefer to eat croissants with butter.
I like to eat my croissant warm with Nutella, strawberries and powdered sugar. Delicious for Easter breakfast or as a dessert!
A croissant with peanut butter and crispy chili flakes!
A croissant from Albert Heijn is always crispy and with the most delicious pistachio filling, this flavour is brilliant for everyone.
I always eat my croissant with Calve peanut butter with chunks and a golden brown spreadable cheese, which I then "dip" it in.
An ultimate combination for a successful croissant; Roasted chicken strips, avocado and siracha mayo. For some extra spice, we'll just pop the filling in!!
Goat cheese, honey and pine nuts. Perfect combination.
A delicious freshly baked croissant with avocado, egg, salmon, herb cream cheese, cucumber and a little salt and pepper. DELICIOUS!
With fresh strawberries on a layer of butter.

A croissant with pure strawberry jam, the two are inextricably linked for me. I do not cut the croissant lengthwise, but make a notch from above. This way the jam stays in the croissant and does not leak from all sides.
A warm, crispy croissant, generously spread with a velvety layer of mascarpone that is subtly scented with lavender — it’s like taking a bite of spring.
Delicious 'just like that' with cheese
herb cream cheese, smoked salmon, cucumber and a little chives or spring onion
The pistachio pasta!!

We discovered it by accident. During the maternity visit last summer we wanted to hand out rusk with mice. Then we discovered that we were 2 rusks short for the guests. We had just been to the store and got croissants for lunch. Why not, cut it in 2, a good lick of butter on it and the blue mice on top. Something we secretly still do sometimes!
Hi
Best when it is still warm with jam, cheese or brie! Delicious!
On a croissant I would put some yummy guacamole, scrambled eggs with fried onions, and fried bacon specks! Best ever!
I identify myself as a croissant and am looking for a partner.
I think they are the tastiest when they are fresh out of the oven! So still nice and warm, and then without anything in between or on top, just like that a lovely warm croissant to eat!
I like it best to cut my croissant open and then put cream on it with strawberries and a little white chocolate! really delicious
I always eat my croissant like a cake, a nice cheesecake cake. I cut it open, spread cream cheese on it and then slices of strawberry! Delicious, a must!
Maybe a strange combination, but spread good green Pesto on the fresh croissant, delicious slices of mozzarella, and cheese shavings. Hmm.
My father and I used to cut open our croissants and put a fried egg in them. They were super tasty and calorie bombs, but unfortunately I don't do that very often anymore. It used to be our thing.
I am very simple. I always put cheese on my croissant, then put it under the sandwich iron. Croisti is what I call it!
I always top my croissant with strawberry jam and a slice of cheese. Delicious when the croissant is fresh out of the oven
I always put chocolate sprinkles on it, goddammit that's insanely tasty, really damn a croissant with chocolate sprinkles is like an angel cumming in your mouth
I love a Big Mac croissant.
I like to eat my croissant with butter, cheese and jam on top! sweet from the jam, salty from the cheese and then the soft crispy croissant when you bite into it delicious!
A tasty croissant deserves only creamy butter, it doesn't need any other toppings, because it is tasty on its own.
A delicious butter croissant is the best with guava jam made by my Brazilian mother ;)
Chicken mango chutney and cucumber delicious!!!!
A croissant with Nutella and fresh strawberries

This coming Easter I'm going to get started on a festive croissant. I'm going to fill it with a creamy lemon-mascarpone filling and mix in some of the AH Excellent Easter Egg Mix for extra crunch and a sweet touch. Then I'll finish the croissant with pastel icing and some cute sugar flowers. Add some edible gold flakes for luxury and the croissant is ready! To top it off, I'll place some small chocolate eggs around it. This will be the perfect, cheerful start to a colorful Easter!

We always make a party of croissants with the kids. We make 2 variations: They can make their own buttercream (AH baking party) and then we add a little coffee with pieces of hazelnut and white chocolate: the moccassant. The other variation is to pipe a layer of buttercream nicely, fresh or frozen raspberries on top and pieces of white chocolate over it. Hmmm!
The tastiest is a fresh lukewarm croissant with a thick layer of butter and (fresh) strawberry jam.
I prefer to eat them with Paturain
What delicious variations I see passing by here but I prefer to eat my croissant without anything on it or with a little bit of butter! delicious because a good croissant needs nothing to be tasty :)
I see a lot of reactions, but not this one. I don't really eat croissants, not even when I'm on holiday in France. But...….. Pay attention.
Delicious with brie and honey. Slices of tomato and cucumber.
The tastiest topping is of course a nice pure sprinkle, but only when it is still warm, because then it melts nicely in! A chocolate roll is nothing compared to it.
I like to put cheese, chicken fillet and egg on it
I top my croissant with delicious herb cheese, spread thickly.

I have always been a big fan of French toast and I like to prepare my croissants like that too! Cut the croissant horizontally, dip it in a mixture of milk, egg and cinnamon and bake it briefly in a pan with butter. Then top it with mascarpone and strawberries or just a layer of sugar. The new AH Pistachio spread also goes well with it. Croissanté!
Croissant wrong
I love to top my croissant with nutella and fresh strawberries. Highly recommended!

Oh yummy a croissant with cottage cheese and strawberries, nice and fresh and sweet and not even that unhealthy! and if we do something yummy "wrong" we spread a layer of butter on it and sprinkle sugar over it!
Of course I prefer to eat a tompouce croissant or a cronaantje, but if I do top it myself, I prefer to add a layer of butter and chocolate sprinkles or delicious jam!

My ultimate croissant topping? A thick layer of whipped salted butter, followed by a swirl of passionfruit curd and as a finishing touch: crumbled caramel sea salt fudge. Soft, salty, sweet and a little sultry – just like a French spring day at its best.   ✨
We like to make a deliciously sweet croissant pastry. Prepare a bag of pastry cream with whipped cream instead of water, spray a layer of it in the croissant and then add fresh strawberries.
Brie and honey on my croissant delicious. I take a bite and immediately start speaking French
A croissant, fresh from the oven with some Lindt chocolate. Let it melt and then enjoy

My favorite croissant is a tiramisu croissant! Cut open a luxury croissant, top with mascarpone, ladyfingers that you first dip in a mixture of coffee and caramel syrup and to finish it off sprinkle with cocoa powder.
I prefer to eat it with a frikandel. Just like a frikandel roll but French style. Je mappel frinkandelcroissant
Best with smoked salmon, cream cheese and freshly cut chives (replace chives with wild garlic during the season)

I have quite a few ideas to make the croissantburn even more delicious. And I really like the pretzel croissant. I would like to win this beanbag for my sweet neighbor boy Novan of 4 years. Because this boy loves croissant and cuddly toys
I enjoy my warm croissant best with a delicious layer of brie and a touch of honey. But honestly, the croissant doesn't need much, because even without topping it is already delicious!
Heat the croissant with a slice of cheese in the air fryer, then top with some cucumber slices for a deliciously fresh meal
Nothing at all, because I don't eat croissants.
Summer croissant: Fresh from the oven, thin slices of strawberry with a thin layer of vanilla custard or vanilla protein pudding. And for the real show-offs you can mix the custard with mascarpone <3
Heat it up in the oven, fill it with Brie and when it comes out of the oven, add some wild flower honey from AH, it tastes really good
I top my croissant with pesto, mozzarella and tomato and then I put it in the oven. Then I add some basil. Very tasty!!!
Butter, spread nice and thick and then white caster sugar, also nice and generous.
Fresh croissant with pastry cream and fresh strawberries, delicious!
I love a croissant with brie and a little honey on top
I always top my croissant with cherry jam!
I prefer to top my croissants with filet american bacon mayonnaise and fried onions

My favorite, just nice and simple, topped with butter with strawberry jam and sometimes with a slice of cheese for a change. But here at home the favorite is mini-croissant with crushed mice or pure chocolate sprinkles.
dark chocolate plus a little sea salt and peanuts
Delicious with strawberry jam
Croissant in a baguette is my go to
Delicious with a slice of cheese and strawberry jam and my kids with fresh strawberries and sugar mmmmm delicious
with a nice slice of cheese
My daughter and I eat our croissant with butter and sugar.
On a croissant that is preferably still a little warm, top it with white chocolate spread and then some fresh raspberries.... soooo the enjoyment starts now
I prefer my croissant with Nutella, but spread it on the bottom of the croissant instead of cutting the croissant open. This keeps the shape of the croissant better.

The very very best thing on a delicious lukewarm croissant is delicious AH butter with the most delicious chocolate sprinkles from AH with the Zaanse houses or the hamsters! and then eat it with the family on a lovely Sunday morning in the sun....that is.....the ultimate croissant experience :-)
I prefer to have my daughter top my croissant with butter and jam, while I sit and wait lazily with my big bottom on the super croissant beanbag.

I love to top my croissant with a combination of creamy brie, fresh figs and a touch of honey. The soft texture of the brie, the sweetness of the figs and the subtle flavour of the honey create a heavenly taste experience that makes every morning special
Young cheese with syrup, very tasty
Fill with homemade Swiss cream and top with fresh strawberries ;)
I love a croissant with a layer of marshmallow fluff and lemon curd on top, deliciously sweet and fresh!
cheese and ham
Top a croissant with chili smoked cheese and a fried sunny-side-up egg with chili flakes on top.
Croissant French toast with lemon curd or red fruit compote and of course a little icing sugar for the looks!

My favorite croissant topping? Lightly salted butter, thin slices of smoked salmon and a dollop of horseradish cream. Topped with some fresh dill. Fresh, creamy and a bit of luxury just what a croissant deserves :)

My ultimate croissant is topped with white chocolate spread, fresh raspberries, crackling sticks and a touch of mint. But if I want to reach my protein goal, I add peanut butter with banana and a little chia seed.

Our Croissant at home (topped Italian style), with green pesto, rocket, raw ham (prosciutto crudo), mozzarella and fresh cherry tomatoes... A delicious aperitif on the side and you'll feel like you're in La Bella Italia!
if there has to be something on it, then a slice of cheese with some chocolate sprinkles.
croissant with siu mai (shumai, Chinese dumplings)
Cut it open and put paprika chips in between.
I like my croissant best with a layer of tuna salad on top. That sounds crazy but it's really true, delicious. I thought a while ago: let me try that, and it was not bad.
Do not cut it open, but spread the bottom with butter and delicious strawberry jam
A layer of mascarpone, fresh strawberries and a drizzle of honey. Simple, sweet and a bit summery   ☀️
Topping a croissant? Are you crazy :) you eat this plain
Croissant with prosciutto, pear and balsamic cream;
Delicious with smoked salmon, lettuce and herb cream cheese.
Brie with Filet Americain
Jam, Cranberry paste, Speculoos all three on top of each other mmmmmm
Crunch and cream croissant: a croissant filled with cheesecake cream, raspberry jam, then topped with chopped AH Easter eggs and speculaas crumbs
with butter, a slice of young cheese and then in the oven for a while... delicious

My ultimate croissant topping? A layer of salted butter, a swirl of lemon curd for that fresh kick, and topped with a sprinkle of grated white chocolate. Sweet, sour, creamy – every bite is a mini-vacation in the South of France. Extra special to me, because my son was born in France… my own little croissant!
Croissant with fried duck liver, Pata Negra, Savora mustard and fig ice cream.... once had it in a restaurant, it was fantastic!
For me, a delicious gluten-free croissant with spreadable cheese, chocolate spread with a grain of coarse sea salt or with a thick layer of butter and chocolate sprinkles with hamsters!
simply delicious with chocolate spread
With butter
I prefer to eat my croissant with veggie fenugreek cheese and egg salad. Mmmmm
Also delicious with clotted cream and homemade strawberry jam for high tea ;)
I like my croissant best with homemade strawberry jam and a creamy cheese on top (like a brie). Of course, it's best fresh out of the oven!
So nice! Croissant delicious with lotus speculoos, banana and cinnamon.
croissant with chocolate spread is delicious but do you know what is even more delicious? croissants with jam and grated cheese. DELICIOUS
Croissants with jam are the best :)
Freshly baked croissant, delicious straight from the hand, but also delicious with fresh strawberries or strawberry jam!
Croissant Camembert. What the heck??
The most delicious croissant is eaten with half a slice of extra mature Beemster cheese, a fried egg and slices of avocado!
My daughters always want the luxury croissants because they are nice and big and then with sprinkles and then just warm them up in the oven! They then enjoy them.....
With pastry cream on top :D
or nothing on it or with peanut butter and chocolate sprinkles!!
The ultimate topping for a croissant is pistachio paste with ice cream cone nuts!
Delicious with strawberries and whipped cream
Nice warm croissant with butter and jam mmm
Hot smoked salmon, cream cheese with chives and a few capers on a crispy croissant – ultimate Easter brunch vibes!
topped with strawberry jam and cheese
topped with a delicious warm Van Dobben croquette, a layer of mustard on top and enjoy!
despite the butter in the croissant, a generous dollop of extra and homemade strawberry jam. Heaven
just a nice croissant without toppings
Personal? Delicious butter croissant with Albert Heijn Strawberry extra jam
I personally think a croissant with jam is really great. Especially when you take a croissant straight out of the oven and then you combine it with some delicious jam. An absolute sensation.
I like to eat them plain and when they are still a bit warm, yum! What I like is to make a croissant carpaccio with truffle mayo, pine nuts, balsamic vinegar, parmesan cheese flakes and rocket
A croissant with just plain butter and real honey!

A croissant is tasty enough on its own, nothing needs to be added! But if something has to be added, a slice of Zaanlander extra mature with a little strawberry jam. If I win it, I think my 2 Bernese Mountain Dogs will use it to lean against!
Delicious with strawberries!
I always top my croissant with a homemade pistachio cream with nougatine nuts, this is a super tasty combination and I really recommend it to everyone :)

A croissant with cream cheese, salmon, cucumber, lettuce and a little bit of red onion with dill dressing is really the favorite here! My bonus son loves croissants and this beanbag would be super fun in his room!

Oh oh, the choice is huge! Already enjoying a croissant with only butter... But what did I discover today? Some melted Easter eggs caramel sea salt and poured them deliciously between my croissant! You don't know what you're missing :) yum!!
I'm going for a croissant with Zaanlander young mature cheese and salami from the fresh produce department and slices of beef tomato and thin slices of cucumber...
I love to top croissants with fresh fruit and dates. It’s the perfect mix of buttery, sweet and juicy.
Oohh that's a tough one   there are so many delicious things on it from jam to brie with walnut and honey
I like to top my croissant with custard and fresh strawberries, finished with a light dusting of icing sugar.
Deliciously topped with soft goat cheese, some honey on top and pieces of walnut and some rocket.
a croissant with pieces of Milka strawberry chocolate and Milka caramel chocolate, put it in the oven for a while and enjoy, but delicious for those with a sweet tooth
Multi-seed croissant with cheese and cucumber is a favorite

I've been in love with the German singer Dennie Christian for 40 years, you'd think what does that have to do with anything, well I'd love to eat a delicious croissant with him and I don't care what's on it!!
Pieces of brie on top with walnuts and honey on top
A croissant with roast beef is really     ! And jam with sugar is also delicious on it
Very cliché but still Zaanlander mature cheese   . With a little butter on top of course.
Croissant Caprese Deluxe
Sorry but a croissant without cheese? Impossible!
A croissant with butter and chocolate sprinkles
My children will definitely be happy with this.
Belgians with captio spring onion broiled onion truffle maio pitoige mai lettuce and roast beef and then more spicy maio and olives and then preffect crozant
A croissant with salted caramel, roasted pecans and a thin slice of brie, very tasty!
I like to top my croissant with matcha cream, fresh strawberries and a touch of black sesame. Very nice exotic taste/combo recommend it to everyone :)

I don't put any toppings on the croissant, but I eat it the French way. Jam (preferably apricots) on the plate and I dip the croissant in it, or I put a little bit of Jam on the croissant with the knife.
A layer of goat cheese, a dollop of lavender honey, a few caramelized purple grapes, and as a finishing touch: a little smoked sea salt.
Fresh sausage in my home baked croissant. Delicious with a little extra spicy mustard.
I'm going for a croissant with a Surinamese touch, namely deliciously topped with my mother's cod! Add a piece of sour and my day can't get any better!
Personally I think mozzarella with tomatoes, basil and a bit of pesto, then in the tosti iron is great. And also Nutella in a croissant in the tosti iron and then strawberry on top!! :)
Just ham cheese or the praline variant from ah but secretly I think the crompouce is the tastiest!
Well, a bit stubborn
A well-baked piece of bavette, beautifully red on the inside on the croissant. Underneath a small layer of truffle jus, topped with some rocket and parmesan cheese.

Okay, brace yourself: my favorite croissant topping is a layer of pistachio cream, topped with fresh raspberries, and then a sprinkle of lemon zest for that fresh kick. It might sound a little crazy, but trust me—it’s a finger-licking good one.

a layer of cream cheese, strawberry slices, a little honey and... crunchy chips! Sweet, salty, crunchy and creamy, it's like experiencing breakfast, lunch and snack in one bite. Not logical, but legendary. Just like an XXL croissant beanbag: nobody asks for it, but once you have it, you'll never want anything else!
A delicious warm croissant with a slice of ham and cheese in between. To finish this off with a croissant with AH Chocopasta as dessert
A delicious croissant with slices of cheese from the deli department, fresh iceberg lettuce and a tomato from AGF and truffle mayonnaise. Enjoy!
A big croissant with 100% peanut butter with chunks. My friends think I'm crazy, but this is really the tastiest and healthiest ;) combination!
There is only 1 correct answer to this. A croissant with Nutella!
A croissant with bacon, cheese, siracha mayo, sesame seeds and cucumber is something I'd love to have!
croissant with Burrata tomato pesto salami Milano in the oven...wooow that's delicious
hmmm Jam I love jam hmmmm
A croissant with caramel paste on both insides. And then in between cut into small pieces Double Gold caramel magnum
An open-cut croissant, with a layer of Nutella spread on both sides and Kinder Bueno crunched on top.
A croissant with strawberries and cream cheese is my favorite!
Lots of chocolate sprinkles
'Enjoy a culinary start with our artisanal croissant'
Filet americain with a poached egg
A normal croissant, with Nutella and banana slices in between. Delicious!
Luxurious croissant filled with brie and strawberries.
A lovely warm Croissant with strawberry Jam on it, to eat together with my Kids, then the jam falls deliciously on your plate with nice sticky hands, yummy
Cut open the croissant while it is still warm, add salted butter and then a little brown sugar on top...enjoy
Delicious with Nutella hazelnut spread and mashed strawberries
Just eat it straight from the fist, that's when it tastes best
The delicious Betuwe strawberry jam from AH's own brand on a croissant that is just warm from the oven is the perfect start of the day for me!
Double devon cream with a little lemon curd mmmmmmmmmmmmmm....

Preferably a lovely warm croissant with pistachio paste on it. Maybe also a good idea to include this in the small bread assortment. It is really a great combination and also fits the pistachio trend!

A lovely warm croissant, a fresh layer of homemade pastry cream on top of a very thin layer of pistachio paste. On top of this, deliciously marinated fresh strawberries (in white caster sugar and lemon juice (fresh)). Let the marinated strawberries drain well in advance and then mmmmmmmm enjoy. PS watch your fingers!
Make a croissant waffle with fruit because yes, I prefer sweet
This is a nice action for us the employees, this can happen more often!
A croissant cut open and with even more calories on it by spreading salted butter on it and then a layer of honey on top! :-D

Every Sunday at grandma and grandpa's started with the smell of freshly baked croissants in their small kitchen. My ultimate croissant topping? Just like grandma did: butter, strawberry jam and a slice of young cheese. Not special perhaps, but to me it is pure magic. Sweet, salty, warm, and full of memories. One bite and I'm sitting there at that old kitchen table again, legs too short for the ground, and crumbs everywhere.

When I really want to indulge, I like to eat my croissant with a generous layer of Nutella, fresh strawberries and raspberries, and a drizzle of white chocolate sauce on top. Topped with some roasted hazelnuts or pistachios for that delicious crunch. Pure indulgence!
I prefer to eat my croissant the old-fashioned way with jam, a truly delicious combination
All those comments here about 'what' you put on a croissant are nice, but 'where' you put it on the croissant is at least as important for the mouthfeel!
The tastiest Poutine on it of course
The tastiest croissant is simply Dutch with chocolate sprinkles. Preferably with a cup of tea or coffee. That makes it complete.
The tastiest is a croissant with strawberry jam, or a savory croissant with cheese and some Italian herbs and then briefly put in the oven.
A crossaint is the most delicious, as warm as possible, as fresh as possible, from the oven. So that it smells delicious and is deliciously crispy! A fresh crossaint does not need anything else.
I prefer to put a little jam on my croissant, nice and simple but still nice.
On Mother's Day I make my mother (and myself) happy with warm croissants filled with homemade pastry cream and fresh strawberries!

Cut your croissant and put a layer of apple syrup on both sides, then soft goat cheese on top. Then you mash a few lotus speculaas cookies and put the crumbs on the goat cheese. Put the roof back on...mmmmmm

I sometimes make a croissant with pulled chicken. First you make the pulled chicken, preferably marinated in spicy pineapple chipotle. Then you cut the croissant open and spread it with a little creme fresch. Finally I always top it off with some slices of pineapple, pickled red onion, lime, coriander and those crumbled tortilla chips. Bon a petit!
I get my croissant
old fashioned with a sausage! delicious
Freshly baked of course and then cream butter young cheese with strawberry jam on top

My ultimate croissant topping? Forget cheese, jam or chocolate; I'm going for the crocosant: a warm, crispy mini-croquette in a fresh croissant, with a lick of coarse mustard. The crispy outside of the croquette, the soft puff pastry and that creamy ragout on the inside... that's comfort food at its very best. Dutch snack meets French finesse; and believe me, you want to taste it!
I just spread chocolate sprinkles on a croissant. DELICIOUS!!
I top it in different ways.
I eat my crossant first spread it with pesto then tomato slices mozzarella slices. Then a little pesto and pine nuts mmm

On a quiet Sunday morning, as the first rays of sunshine enter the house, the smell of a fresh croissant fills my nose. The outside is golden brown and crispy, but the best part is yet to come: the topping. A wafer-thin slice of cheese melts gently on the warm bread, while I place a perfectly fried egg on top. A little bit of butter for creaminess completes the picture. The taste is a combination of rich, soft and savoury, which makes every bite extra delicious. As I eat it calmly, with a cup of coffee within reach, I feel completely at ease. It is simple, but so surprisingly delicious!
Just a nice slice of old Zaanlander cheese!
